rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DYKES VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANTs RUSSELL B. FLYNN INVESTMENT COMPANY L.L.C., and RUSSELL B. FLYNN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication GRANTED. Defendants shifted their burden. Plaintiff failed to present competent admissible evidence to show that a triable issue of fact exists. Plaintiff's request to continue under CCP 437c is denied. This request is not supported by affidavits. Additionally, discovery information from Plaintiff's own medical provider and from the Assessor's Office has been available to Plaintiff since this case was filed on February 10, 2016 and Plaintiff was served with the Motion for Summary Judgment on May 1, 2018, i.e. 2.5 months before opposition was due. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT DYKES VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Extend Time Of Response To Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF SCOTT DYKES' Motion To Extend Time Of Response To Summary Judgment OFF CALENDAR, untimely. This motion will be construed as a request to continue under CCP 437c(h). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: OREGON MUTUAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. BRUCE I. GILBERT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT SUNSET SCAVENGER COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT continued to August 30, 2018. Moving party is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers, operative complaint and reply to department 501 no later than August 10, 2018 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID BANYARD ET AL VS. PIERRE STEPHAN ANDRADA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 3. DEFENDANT PIERRE ANDRADA's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a claim under Probate Code 9391. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER MASSIS VS. JEREMY BRIGGS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jeremy Biggs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Nimer Massis And His Attorney Of Record, Frank Kim, In The Amount Of $942
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 4. DEFENDANTs JEREMY BIGGS, and ANGELA BIGGS' Motion To Compel Site Inspection; Request For Sanctions Against Plaintiff Nimer Massis And His Attorney Of Record, Frank Kim, In The Amount Of $942 GRANTED. No opposition filed. Inspection to proceed on a mutually agreeable date no later than August 10, 2018. If parties cannot agree on a date, inspection shall be on August 8, 2018. Sanctions granted in the amount of $942. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 1725 TURK I7, LP VS. EVA EASTMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motiion To Quash Service Of Summons & Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 31, 2018 line 8. DEFENDANT EVA EASTMAN's Motiion To Quash Service Of Summons & Complaint DENIED. Defendant was properly sub-served by a registered process server. The Court notes that this motion was filed and set for hearing in violation of CCP 1167.4 and that the declaration by a person sub-served was not filed with the moving papers, i.e. competent evidence was provided only with reply. The motion and the declaration were nevertheless considered. Substitute service on the mother of the Defendant, a person who answered and opened the door accomplishes the purpose of the substitute service statute. "The evident purpose of Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20 is to permit service to be completed upon a good faith attempt at physical service on a responsible person...." Service must be made upon a person whose "relationship with the person to be served makes it more likely than not that they will deliver process to the named party." Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393-1394 citing 50 Court St. Assoc. v. Mendelson et al. (1991) 151 Misc.2d 87. Here, the mother's relationship with defendant made it more likely than not that she would deliver process to defendant. The Court notes Defendant does not claim she failed to receive notice of service. = (501/REQ)
